name: Christmas Tree Cakes
recipeIngredient: 1 ½ packages Softened cream cheese (8 oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease a 15 x 10 x 1-inch jelly roll pan and line it with waxed paper.
3. Prepare the cake mix according to package directions for the "lite" recipe, incorporating the orange peel.
4. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 20 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean.
5. Allow the cake to cool in the pan for at least 15 minutes.
6. Invert the cake onto a board covered with waxed paper, lifting it from the pan, and peel away any waxed paper from the bottom of the cake.
7. While the cake cools completely, cut triangle patterns on lightweight cardboard using various sizes (e.g., 4-inch base, 5½-inch sides; 3-inch base, 4-inch sides; 2½-inch base, 3½-inch sides; etc.).
8. Using the cardboard patterns to fit and turn as necessary, carefully cut triangles from the cake.
9. In a large bowl with the mixer on medium speed, beat together the butter, cream cheese, and vanilla until light and fluffy.
10. Gradually beat in the confectioners' sugar until thoroughly blended and smooth.
11. Remove about half of the frosting and stir in green food coloring to achieve the desired shade.
12. Spread the remaining frosting over the top and sides of each cake triangle using a flexible metal spatula, smoothing it out or swirling gently to resemble tree branches.
13. Decorate the cakes as desired with candies, colored sugars, assorted sprinkles, and nonpareils.
14. Using the tip of a small knife, gently hollow out a small area in the center of the bottom edge of each cake for trunks; insert caramel rolls or licorice sticks.
